title: Newborn glomerular function and gestational particulate air pollution.
ehr_evidence: The study uses the ENVIRONAGE cohort and a Dutch questionnaire; no EHR or linked health data are mentioned.
summary: The study evaluates the impact of gestational particulate air pollution exposure (black carbon and PM2.5) on cord blood cystatin C levels, which serve as a marker for glomerular function in newborns.
key_findings: Every 0.5 μg/m³ and 5 μg/m³ increment in gestational exposure to black carbon (BC) and fine particulate matter (PM2.5) corresponded to increases of 0.04 mg/L and 0.07 mg/L in cord blood cystatin C levels, respectively (p < 0.01).
Third-trimester exposure showed similar associations with increased cystatin C levels.
